response.justification: Sarah Smith is currently a Staff engineer at re:cinq working directly with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators—exactly the technical stack and responsibilities this role requires. She has 19 years of software engineering experience, proven staff-level leadership, and strong TypeScript/cloud (AWS) expertise. However, the resume lacks evidence of Python expertise (a primary requirement), demonstrates limited depth in vector databases, RAG architectures, and LLM-specific knowledge, and her AI experience is concentrated in a very recent role (1 month at re:cinq). Her current work is promising but requires deeper vetting to confirm production AI systems experience.
strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at staff level—demonstrating hands-on expertise in the exact technologies and workflows this role demands.

19 years of software engineering with proven staff-level technical leadership across multiple organizations, plus evidence of mentorship and knowledge-sharing through workshops and community contributions.
Strong TypeScript, Node.js, and AWS cloud platform experience with demonstrated ability to architect production systems handling significant scale (millions of requests, large-scale optimization).
concerns: No evidence of Python expertise despite it being a primary required language; resume lists many languages but Python is conspicuously absent for an AI-focused staff role.
Limited explicit mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI), or LLM performance metrics—core competencies for this position.

AI systems experience appears concentrated in current role started April 2025 (1 month tenure); insufficient track record to assess depth of production GenAI delivery or ability to mentor others in AI/ML domains.

key_factors.factor: Current AI-agent and MCP experience at Staff level
Extensive distributed systems and cloud background
